titlepage.titleproper: Finding Aid for the Stanley A. Wolpert Papers, 1960-1962
titlepage.publisher:
UCLA Library, Department of Special Collections
Manuscripts Division
Los Angeles, CA

list.head: Contact Information
list.item: UCLA Library, Department of Special Collections
Room A1713, Charles E. Young Research Library

defitem.label: Text converted and initial container list EAD tagging by:

titlepage.p: © 2003 The Regents of the University of California. All rights reserved.

did.head: Descriptive Summary

repository.label: Repository
repository.corpname: University of California, Los Angeles. Library. Department of Special Collections.

did.abstract: Stanley Albert Wolpert was a professor of history at UCLA, and served as the department chair (1968- ). The collection consists of an early typescript with holograph corrections, galley proofs and a revised typescript with extensive holograph and editorial corrections of Wolpert's novel Nine hours to Rama.
did.physloc: Stored off-site at SRLF. Advance notice is required for access to the collection. Please contact the UCLA Library, Department of Special Collections Reference Desk for paging information.

userestrict.head: Restrictions on Use and Reproduction
userestrict.p: Property rights to the physical object belong to the UCLA Library, Department of Special Collections. Literary rights, including copyright, are retained by the creators and their heirs. It is the responsibility of the researcher to determine who holds the copyright and pursue the copyright owner or his or her heir for permission to publish where The UC Regents do not hold the copyright.

accessrestrict.head: Restrictions on Access
accessrestrict.p: COLLECTION STORED OFF-SITE AT SRLF: Advance notice required for access.

altformavail.head: Additional Physical Form Available
altformavail.p: A copy of the original version of this online finding aid is available at the UCLA Department of Special Collections for in-house consultation and may be obtained for a fee. Please contact:

list.item: UCLA Library, Department of Special Collections
Room A1713, Charles E. Young Research Library

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: [Identification of item], Stanley A. Wolpert Papers (Collection 768). Department of Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, University of California, Los Angeles.

bioghist.head: Biography
bioghist.p: Stanley Albert Wolpert was born on December 23, 1927 in Brooklyn, New York; BA, City College of New York, 1953; AM (1955) and Ph.D (1959), University of Pennsylvania; instructor (1959-60), assistant professor (1960-63), associate professor (1963-66), and in 1967 professor of history, UCLA; also served, beginning in 1968, as department chair; won Distinguished Teaching Award, UCLA, 1975; published books include Aboard the flying swan (1954), Tilak and Gokhale: revolution and reform in the making of modern India (1962), Nine hours to Rama (1962), India (1965), Morley and India, 1906-1910 (1967), The expedition (1968), An error of judgment (1970), A new history of India (1976), Roots of confrontation in South Asia: Afghanistan, Pakistan, India, and the Superpowers (1982), Jinnah of Pakistan: a life (1984), and Nehru: a tryst with destiny (1996).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Collection consists of galley proofs and a revised typescript with extensive holograph and editorial corrections of Stanley Wolpert's novel Nine hours to Rama. Includes the typescript with holograph corrections of the same novel under its original title, Day of darkness.

controlaccess.head: Indexing Terms
controlaccess.p: The following terms have been used to index the description of this collection in the library's online public access catalog.
controlaccess.corpname: University of California, Los Angeles--Department of History--Faculty--Archival resources.

did.unittitle: Wolpert, Stanley. A day of darkness. Los Angeles, ca. 1960.

list.item: Retitled Nine hours to Rama, and published by Random House, New York (1962).

did.unittitle: --- ---- Another copy. New York: Random House, 1962.

list.item: This is the copy from which type was set.
